Missing Persons Report Mitras PD — File 01 — Capt. Brzenska
Case Header
Status:Active / Missing Person
Detective in Charge:Capt. Rico Brzenska
Date Filed: 
Case Type:Possible Circumstantial / Foul Play
1. Victim Profile
Subject Photo
File Copy
Name:Historia D. Reiss
Age at Disappearance:18 at time of disappearance
DOB:January 15, 1970
Address: 
Occupation:Student, Aurelius Preparatory Academy
Physical Description
Description:Blonde hair, blue eyes, fair complexion, slender build.
Identifying Marks:Beauty mark, left shoulder.
Last Seen Wearing: 
2. Circumstances of Disappearance
Date / Time Last Seen: 
Location Last Seen:Aurelius Preparatory Academy / surrounding transit routes
Subject was reported missing after failing to return home and ceasing all contact with family and known friends. Initial reporting was delayed due to conflicting accounts regarding her intended destination after school hours.
Last Known Action: Multiple witnesses place Reiss on school grounds shortly before disappearance. Timeline becomes inconsistent thereafter.
3. Vehicle / Assets
Vehicle:None reported.
Phone Status:No confirmed activity after disappearance.
Financial Activity:No verified activity after initial report.
Unverified CCTV still
Authenticity not confirmed
Is it her —
or a lookalike? — R.B.
No trace. Almost smells like the work of "the Ghost of Mitras" — but is it??? — R.B.
Tip came in anonymous —
can't verify the source.
Could be a dead end. — R.B.
4. Investigative Leads
Anonymous tip alleged a possible sighting in Stohess two years after initial disappearance.
No trace of subject despite extended search efforts.
CCTV image submitted separately; authenticity not verified.
5. Known Friends / Witnesses
 
 
 
6. Initial Findings
Disappearance does not presently support a clean voluntary departure. Lack of verified contact, missing timeline consistency, and later alleged sighting suggest either concealment, misdirection, or third-party involvement.

Missing Persons Report Mitras PD — File 02 — Capt. Brzenska
Case Header
Status:Active / Missing Person
Detective in Charge:Capt. Rico Brzenska
Date Filed:March 12, 2026
Case Type:Possible Circumstantial / Foul Play
1. Victim Profile
Subject Photo
File Copy
Name:Forster, Floch B.
Age at Disappearance:17 at time of disappearance
DOB:October 8, 1970
Address: 
Occupation:Student
Physical Description
Description:Red hair, light eyes, fair complexion, lean build.
Identifying Marks:Small scar on right ear.
Last Seen Wearing: 
2. Circumstances of Disappearance
Date / Time Last Seen: 
Location Last Seen:Mitras entertainment district
Subject was last reliably placed leaving an evening social function. Follow-up witness statements diverged significantly on route, companions, and intended destination.
Last Known Action: Departed venue area on foot. No confirmed visual after that point.
3. Vehicle / Assets
Vehicle:None reported.
Phone Status:No verified answer or activity after disappearance.
Financial Activity:No confirmed post-disappearance use.
Evidence Photo — Item #1
Human molar, alley drainage
Single tooth — but where is HE!!! — R.B.
Sounds like the Butcher's work perhaps... — R.B.
4. Investigative Leads
Single human molar recovered near alley drainage close to the boonies.
No additional remains or recoverable blood pool found at scene.
File informally compared to other disappearances with incomplete remains and inconsistent witness accounts.
5. Known Friends / Witnesses
 
 
 
6. Initial Findings
Case does not support voluntary disappearance. Recovery of isolated human tooth with no complete scene suggests interruption, transport, or staged disposal. Possibility of homicide remains high.

Missing Persons Report Mitras PD — File 03 — Capt. Brzenska
Case Header
Status:Active / Missing Person
Detective in Charge:Det. Evelyn Carter — moved under Capt. R. Brzenska
Date Filed:March 12, 2026
Case Type:Open / Missing
1. Victim Profile
Subject Photo
File Copy
Name:Akari, Ritsuko R.
Age at Disappearance:34 at time of disappearance
DOB: 
Address: 
Occupation:Pharmacist
Physical Description
Description:Adult female, blonde hair (possibly natural brunette), light complexion, average build.
Identifying Marks:Birthmark on left cheekbone.
Last Seen Wearing:Purple button-up, black pencil skirt, low black pumps, white lab coat.
2. Circumstances of Disappearance
Date / Time Last Seen: 
Location Last Seen:East Mitras commuter stop
Subject left work at normal time and failed to return home. Personal purse was later recovered from a public bench with no overt signs of struggle in the immediate area.
Last Known Action: Observed leaving workplace alone.
3. Vehicle / Assets
Vehicle:Cream Toyota Camry, 1991.
Phone Status:No reply to repeated contact attempts.
Financial Activity:No confirmed unusual activity.
Evidence Item #1
Subject's purse — recovered on public bench, East Mitras commuter stop
Too clean — voluntary or not,
that is the question. — R.B.
4. Investigative Leads
Purse recovered intact.
No confirmed CCTV exit trail.
No immediate connection to other open cases officially established.
5. Known Friends / Witnesses
Coworkers — no report of unusual behavior prior to disappearance.
Family contacts — no post-disappearance communication.
6. Initial Findings
Disappearance remains open. Lack of visible struggle may indicate controlled removal, or voluntary meeting with unknown party.
Missing
Missing Persons Report Mitras PD — File 04 — Capt. Brzenska
Case Header
Status:Active / Missing Person
Detective in Charge:Det. Evelyn Carter — moved under Capt. R. Brzenska
Date Filed:March 12, 2026
Case Type:Open / Missing
1. Victim Profile
Subject Photo
File Copy
Name:Miller, Joel L.
Age at Disappearance:58 at time of disappearance
DOB: 
Address: 
Occupation:Dockworker / shift labor
Physical Description
Description:Older male, dark hair with graying, rugged build.
Identifying Marks:Scars on hands and arms.
Last Seen Wearing:Blue jeans, navy flannel shirt, dark boots.
2. Circumstances of Disappearance
Date / Time Last Seen: 
Location Last Seen:Dockyards
Employer reported subject missing after failure to appear for three consecutive shifts. No confirmed contact with workplace after last known check-out. Vehicle left in a lot a few blocks from the dockyard.
Last Known Action: Departed work area alone.
3. Vehicle / Assets
Vehicle:Ford F-150, 1991 — left in lot approx. two blocks from last known location.
Phone Status:Unreachable.
Financial Activity:No reliable activity after disappearance.
Evidence Photo
Subject's vehicle — Ford F-150, recovered in lot near dockyards
Why leave the truck?
Man like this doesn't
walk anywhere. — R.B.
Debt history —
check loan records
& known creditors. — R.B.
4. Investigative Leads
Employment attendance pattern broke abruptly.
Debt history complicated early screening of motives.
No confirmed trace route after last shift.
5. Known Friends / Witnesses
Employer — filed initial concern after repeated absence.
Coworkers — no confirmed unusual dispute immediately prior; said he kept to himself.
6. Initial Findings
Lack of reliable exit trail or financial activity leaves voluntary disappearance unconfirmed. Possibility of opportunistic foul play not excluded.

Name:Spiegel, Spike G.
Age at Disappearance:27 at time of disappearance
DOB: 
Address: 
Occupation:Independent contractor
Physical Description
Description:Adult male, dark hair, brown eyes, lean build.
Identifying Marks:Small scar on left eyelid.
Last Seen Wearing:Blue business suit, yellow button-up shirt.
2. Circumstances of Disappearance
Date / Time Last Seen: 
Location Last Seen:Mitras South District
Subject last seen leaving a late-night establishment near south transit routes. Witness statements conflicted on condition, companions, and direction of travel.
Last Known Action: Left venue area shortly before records go dark.
3. Vehicle / Assets
Vehicle: 
Phone Status:No confirmed follow-up activity.
Financial Activity:No reliable transactions after disappearance.
Last Known Location
South District late-night establishment, Mitras
"Ghost of Mitras"
perhaps??? — R.B.
Too staged...
can't be too sure. — R.B.
4. Investigative Leads
Witness accounts appear contaminated by alcohol influence and inconsistent timelines.
No usable exit footage from immediate area.
Case inactivity attributed to weak corroboration, not resolution.
5. Known Friends / Witnesses
Bar staff — unable to provide reliable final contact window.
Patrons — contradictory descriptions of subject's departure.
6. Initial Findings
Case remains unresolved. Poor witness quality and lack of exit trace raise possibility that disappearance scene was staged or indicative of abduction.
